revision.comment: Bot: remove [[Category:Sn crud]] (crud category cleanup) [[github:EmmaLeonhart/shintowiki-scripts/actions/runs/23984597483|manual triggered pipeline]]
revision.text:
[[ja:江崎古墳]] {{Infobox ancient site|coordinates={{Coord|34.669167|N|133.776944|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}|architectural_styles={{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|qid=Q11268718|1=Zenpōkōenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳}}}} {{Ancient_Kofun_in_Japan |name=Ezaki Kofun |image=[[File:Ezaki Kofun, funkyu.jpg|280px]]
Mound
(rear circular section and entrance on the right, front section in the back left) |location={{ill|Okayama Prefecture|en|Okayama Prefecture|qid=Q132936|lt=Okayama Prefecture|1=Okayama Prefecture}}{{ill|Sōja|en|Sōja|qid=Q873536|lt=Sōja, Okayama|1=Sōja}} Kamibayashi, Ezaki |coordinates={{Coord|34|40|6.72|N|133|46|36.90|E}} |type={{ill|Zenpokoenfun|en|Zenpokoenfun|qid=Q11268718|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpōkōenfun}} |map_size=Mound length: 45m |Burial Facility={{ill|Yokoana|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokoana|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} with an internal house-shaped sarcophagus |built=Late {{ill|6th century|en|6th century|qid=Q8090|lt=6th century|1=6th century}} to early {{ill|7th century|en|7th century|qid=Q8089|lt=7th century|1=7th century}} |burial goods=Iron-co11er horse tra11ings, iron swords, and {{ill|Sue pottery|en|Sue pottery|qid=Q7634351|lt=Sue pottery|1=Sue pottery}} |Historic Site={{ill|Okayama Prefecture Designated Historic Sites|ja|岡山県指定文化財一覧#史跡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Okayama Prefecture Designated Historic Sites|1=Okayama Prefecture Designated Historic Sites|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11472269}}, designated in 1986 |image=[[File:Ezaki Kofun sekishitsu.jpg|thumb|280px|right|Interior of the burial chamber]] }} '''Ezaki Kofun''' (江崎古墳) is a {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|en|Zenpokoenfun|qid=Q11268718|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpōkōenfun}} located in {{ill|Sōja|en|Sōja|qid=Q873536|lt=Sōja, Okayama|1=Sōja}}, Kamibayashi, Ezaki, Okayama Prefecture. It is designated as a Historic Site of Okayama Prefecture. The site was excavated in 1984 by Yoshirō Kondō and his team from Okayama University, revealing a large {{ill|yokoana|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/yokoana|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} (horizontal stone chamber) with a house-shaped sarcophagus. Numerous artifacts, including horse tra11ings and weapons, were unearthed during the investigation. == Overview == === Mound === The tumulus was constructed on a slope with the front section oriented uphill and the rear circular section downhill, aligned along a north-south axis. It is a two-tier structure measuring 45 meters in total length, with the rear circular section having a diameter of approximately 32 meters and a height of 5.2 meters. The front rectangular section measures 25 meters in width and 1.2 meters in height. A moat, ranging from 3.0 to 5.8 meters in width and 0.8 to 1.6 meters in depth, encircles the mound from the rear circular section to the front section. Fragments of haniwa were sparsely found around the front, neck, and rear circular sections. These included cylindrical haniwa, morning-glory-shaped haniwa, and figurative haniwa. Only one example of Sue pottery was identified among the fragments. === Burial Chamber === The burial chamber is located slightly southward of the rear circular section's center, with an entrance facing west. The chamber is a dual-sleeved horizontal stone structure made of large granite blocks arranged in two to three layers, with gaps filled by smaller stones. The rear wall is a single massive granite slab. Most of the ceiling stones were lost except for three near the entrance. The dimensions of the chamber are as follows: * Total length: 13.8 meters * Main chamber: ** Length: 6.6 meters ** Width: 2.6 meters (at the rear wall) to 2.3 meters (at the sleeves) ** Height: 2.9 meters (rear wall) to 2.66 meters (to the existing ceiling stones) * Corridor: ** Length: 1.7 meters ** Height: 2.5 meters * Entrance: Height of 1.9 meters The burial chamber has a floor area of 16 square meters, roughly 60% the size of the floor area of {{ill|Kōmorizuka Kofun|en|Kōmorizuka Kofun|qid=Q11266204|lt=Kōmorizuka Kofun|1=Kōmorizuka Kofun}}. === Sarcophagus === The central burial chamber houses a carved house-shaped sarcophagus near the right wall. The lid features small, flat cord-fastening protrusions, which are non-functional. The southeast corner of the lid is damaged, suggesting looting. The sarcophagus dimensions are: * Base: ** Length: 238 cm ** Width: 125 cm ** Height: 68 cm * Lid: ** Length: 251 cm ** Width: 143 cm ** Height: 43 cm The stone material is shell limestone, identical to that used for the sarcophagus in {{ill|Kōmorizuka Kofun|en|Kōmorizuka Kofun|qid=Q11266204|lt=Kōmorizuka Kofun|1=Kōmorizuka Kofun}}. === Artifacts and Burial Goods === Two human remains were found in the sarcophagus. One was a male estimated to be 20–30 years old, with a height of 156–157 cm. The other was a female aged 30–40 years, of a similar height. Artifacts found included: * Horse tra11ings: 30 decorative fittings, fragments of a ring bit, and a bridle ornament. * Weapons: Five iron swords, 132 iron arrowheads, and four iron knives. * Ornaments: Approximately110 glass beads (blue and brown, 4–5 mm in diameter), one pair of gold rings. * Pottery: 80 Sue pottery pieces (high-footed cups, li11ed vessels, long-necked jars, etc.), along with Haji pottery. A bronze mirror (14.1 cm in diameter) with a beast design and various other items were also unearthed. == Chronology == Based on the Sue pottery, Ezaki Kofun dates to the late 6th century to early 7th century, slightly later than {{ill|Kōmorizuka Kofun|en|Kōmorizuka Kofun|qid=Q11266204|lt=Kōmorizuka Kofun|1=Kōmorizuka Kofun}}. Evidence of later reuse was identified, including a double-handled jar from the Heian period containing cremated remains, suggesting the chamber was repurposed for burial during that era. == Cultural Properties == === Okayama Prefecture Designated Cultural Properties === * Historic Site ** Ezaki Kofun - Designated on April 4, 1986[http://www.pref.okayama.jp/cgi-bin/bunka/culture/controller/client/resultDetail.cgi?id=351 Ezaki Kofun] (Okayama Prefecture “Cultural Properties of Okayama”).. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * Kondō, Yoshirō et al. "Ezaki Kofun." In ''Sōja City Historical Archaeological Materials.'' Sōja City Historical Compilation Committee, 1987, 11. 272–283. * Niino, Izumi. "Ezaki Kofun, Kamibayashi, Sōja City, Okayama Prefecture." In ''Archaeological Research, Volume 31, Issue 4,'' Archaeological Research Association, March 1985, p. 7. == See Also == * {{ill|Kōmorizuka Kofun|en|Kōmorizuka Kofun|qid=Q11266204|lt=Kōmorizuka Kofun|1=Kōmorizuka Kofun}} * {{ill|Tobiotsuka Kofun|ja|鳶尾塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tobiotsuka Kofun|qq=}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Ezaki Kofun}} * [http://www.pref.okayama.jp/cgi-bin/bunka/culture/controller/client/resultDetail.cgi?id=351 Ezaki Kofun] - Okayama Prefecture “Cultural Properties of Okayama” * [http://www.city.soja.okayama.jp/bunka/kanko/shitei_bunkazai/ken/ken_13.html Ezaki Kofun] - Sōja City “Sōja Sightseeing Guide” {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|江崎古墳|version=100224879|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:江崎古墳]] {{Commons category|Ezaki Kofun}} {{wikidata link|Q11550858}} [[Category:Designated historic sites of Okayama Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Sōja, Okayama]] [[Category:Kofun of Okayama prefecture]] [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]]
